function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
Is there a call path from function 'main' to function 'printf'?
loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'x >= 0' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'x >= 0' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'y >= 0' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'y >= 0' always executed?
dominators: Does the statement line 21:  <statement> for( ; y >= 0; y-- ) </statement> dominate line 17:  <statement> printf( "y-- = [%d]\n", y-- ); </statement>?
Does the statement line 9:  <statement> printf( "x = [%d]\n", x ); </statement> dominate line 11:  <statement> for( ; x >= 0; --x ) </statement>?
data_dependency: Does variable y at line 5: <statement> 	int y = 5; </statement> determine the value stored in variable y at line 17: <statement> 	printf( "y-- = [%d]\n", y-- ); </statement>?
Does the value of variable y at line 17: <statement> 	printf( "y-- = [%d]\n", y-- ); </statement> depend on the value of variable x at line 11: <statement> 	for( ; x >= 0; --x ) </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'x' live at the end of function 'main'?
Does variable 'y' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the program actually reach the statement: “printf( "--x = [%d]\n", --x );”?
